title: Card-Not-Present Transaction Spikes
spl:
index=payments sourcetype="gateway:txn" card_present="false"
| timechart span=1h sum(amount) as gmv, count as txns
| eventstats avg(txns) as baseline, stdev(txns) as s
| eval z=(txns-baseline)/s
| where abs(z)>3
description: E-commerce CNP spikes often precede credential stuffing or BIN attacks.
value: E-commerce CNP spikes often precede credential stuffing or BIN attacks.
implementation: Segment by merchant and MCC; seasonal baselines for retail peaks. Correlate with 3DS failures and chargeback velocity.
visualization: Line chart (CNP volume vs. baseline), Single value (Z-score), Table (merchants over threshold).
knownFalsePositives: E-commerce and recurring-subscription CNP surges, flash sales, or gateway retries; seasonality and marketing calendars explain many patterns.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Gateway TA, e-commerce platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="gateway:txn"`, `card_present="false"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Segment by merchant and MCC; seasonal baselines for retail peaks. Correlate with 3DS failures and chargeback velocity.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="gateway:txn" card_present="false"
| timechart span=1h sum(amount) as gmv, count as txns
| eventstats avg(txns) as baseline, stdev(txns) as s
| eval z=(txns-baseline)/s
| where abs(z)>3
```

Understanding this SPL

**Card-Not-Present Transaction Spikes** — E-commerce CNP spikes often precede credential stuffing or BIN attacks.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="gateway:txn"`, `card_present="false"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Gateway TA, e-commerce platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: gateway:txn. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="gateway:txn". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **z** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where abs(z)>3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (CNP volume vs. baseline), Single value (Z-score), Table (merchants over threshold).
grandmaExplanation: In Financial Services, we pay close attention to "Card-Not-Present Transaction Spikes" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
